etymology.description: semantic 貝(貝) (bèi, "shell, money") + phonetic 易(易) (yì, "filled container") -> bestow
etymology.interpretation: 貝(貝) (bèi, "shell, money") symbolizes valuables or treasures, while 易(易) (yì, "filled container") provides the sound. Together, they depict the act of granting or bestowing valuables or benefits onto someone, which is closely associated with giving gifts or rewards.
etymology.simplification: In the simplified form 赐(賜), the component 貝(貝) (bèi) was simplified to 贝(貝), reducing the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 国王赐给了他一件珍贵的礼物(國王賜給了他一件珍貴的禮物)。
examples.pinyin: Guówáng cì gěi le tā yī jiàn zhēnguì de lǐwù.
examples.english: The king bestowed upon him a precious gift.

examples.chinese: 神明赐予我们好运(神明賜予我們好運)。
examples.english: The deities bestowed good fortune upon us.

examples.chinese: 他希望上天能赐给他智慧(他希望上天能賜給他智慧)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xīwàng shàngtiān néng cì gěi tā zhìhuì.
examples.english: He hopes that the heavens will bestow wisdom upon him.
